english: lhág-tu rtsis-su byed (lhag tu rtsis su byed) he respected her beyond measure Tar., Schf. — *si-rúg* (*si rug*) vulgo W. for rtsis (rtsis) in most of its significations.

1. also rtsis-mkan (rtsis mkhan) mathematician, astronomer, soothsayer; accountant Cs. — 2. n. pr. rtsis-pa a-mgrón (rtsis pa a mgron) secular, rtsis-pa mgron-gnyér (rtsis pa mgron gnyer) spiritual name of the late Resident of the Sikim government at Darjeeling, called by the English Cheboo Lama, † 1866, v. Hooker Journ. — rtsis-dpon (rtsis dpon) a chief mathematician, chief accountant, receiver general Cs.

I. vb. to revile, abuse, v. rṅor rtsub-pa (rngor rtsub pa) sub ṅo (ngo). II. adj., com. rtsúb-po (rtsub po), rtsúb-mo (rtsub mo) Ssk. परुष, uneven, rough, rugged, of the skin, cloth etc.; coarse-grained, powder; rough, wild, dreary, countries, roṅ-rtsub (rong rtsub) with wild ravines Glr.; bristly, hair; harsh, tart, astringent, of taste Med.; also applied to any thing of a highly aromatic, pricking, pungent or acerb taste, such as onions and similar vegetables, liable to cause both dietetic and religious scruples; rtsub-zás (rtsub zas) food of this description; in music: strong, forte; of sentiment and behaviour: rude, unfeeling, regardless, callous S.g., Glr.

1. point, top, peak, summit, kaṅ- (khang-), gri- (gri-), ri- (ri-), śiṅ-rtse (shing rtse), or kaṅ-pai (khang pa'i) etc., rtsé-mo (rtse mo) gable of a house, point of a knife, top of a hill, head of a tree; of convents, royal palaces, resp.: dbú-rtse (dbu rtse) Glr.; lá-rtse (la rtse), W. *lár-se* (*lar se*) (cf. rtsá-ba (rtsa ba) I, 2.) *lá-se* (*la se*) summit of a mountain-pass; rtse daṅ logs-su (rtse dang logs su) terminal and lateral Wdṅ.; rtsé-sgro (rtse sgro) Glr. flag-feather, pinion; źa rtse-riṅ (zhwa rtse ring) hat with a high crown Tar.; rtse γcil-ba (rtse gcil ba) Sch.: to break off the point, to blunt; rtse-reg-čé (rtse reg ce) Mil. very sensitive, touchy, not to be touched with the tip of the finger. — 2. point, particular spot, rtse γcig-tu ltá-ba (rtse gcig tu lta ba) to look at one point; also adv., to look steadily, unremittingly, as: raṅ-gi grib-ma-la rtse-γcig-tu ltá-ba (rang gi grib ma la rtse gcig tu lta ba) Wdṅ., also Tar. frq.; sems rtse γcig-tu byed-pa (sems rtse gcig tu byed pa) to direct the mind to one point, frq.; sems rtse-γcig-tu byás-pai tiṅ-ṅe-°dzin-la źugs-te (sems rtse gcig tu byas pa'i ting nge 'dzin la zhugs te) Dzl.; aim, tse °dii rtse γcig (tshe 'di'i rtse gcig) as this life's only aim Mil.

pf. rtses (rtses), imp. rtse(s) (rtse (s)), क्रीड़, (different from brtsé-ba (brtse ba)) 1. to play, mig-maṅ (mig mang) at chess Dzl.; to sport, to frolic, used also of animals Dzl.; rtse bro γtóṅ-ba (rtse bro gtong ba) to run to and fro, playing and skipping, of deer Mil.; to joke, to jest, rtsé-źiṅ dgá-ba (rtse zhing dga' ba), rtse-dgá spyód-pa (rtse dga' spyod pa) id.; *yáṅ(s)-pa sé-cè* (*yang (s) pa se ce*) W. id.; to enjoy, amuse, divert one's self, to take recreation, tsal-gyi naṅ-du rtser soṅ (tshal gyi nang du rtser song) they went on a pleasure party into the woods Dzl.; euphem. of cohabitation, °di daṅ rtsé-bar byao ('di dang rtse bar bya'o) Pth. I mean to enjoy her. Comp. rtsé-mkan (rtse mkhan) player, gambler, gamester. — rtse-grógs (rtse grogs), rtsed-grógs (rtsed grogs) play-mate. — rtse-dgá (rtse dga') v. above. — rtse-rgód (rtse rgod) sport and laughter. — rtsé-sa (rtse sa) play-ground, place of amusement. — rtse-sems (rtse sems) a mind fond of play; kyód-kyis rtsé-sems yin mod kyaṅ (khyod kyis rtse sems yin mod kyang) though you may still relish pleasures Pth. 2. to touch, W. *lág-pa ma se* (*lag pa ma se*) do not touch it with your hand. — 3. to shudder (cf. spu (spu)).

pf. (b)rtsegs ((b)rtsegs), fut. brtseg (brtseg), imp. rtsog (rtsog), W. *ság-cè(s)* (*sag ce (s)*) 1. to lay one thing on or over another, to pile up, stack up, wood, boards; to put slices of meat on bread; fig.: ná-ro γnyis brtseg (na ro gnyis brtseg) two 'naro' one above the other, ཽ, Gram.; gen. double; kaṅ-pa rtsegs-pa (khang pa rtsegs pa) 1. 'a house of two stories' = a stately building, palace; by this word Wdṅ. explains kaṅ-bzáṅ (khang bzang), v. bzáṅ-po (bzang po). 2. acc. to other Lexx., an apartment built on another, an upper chamber; balcony on the roof of a house, कूटागार; rgya-grám brtségs-pai mčód-rten (rgya gram brtsegs pa'i mchod rten) a chod-ten with a cross (v. rgya-grám (rgya gram)) on the top Pth. — 2. to tuck up, clothes Cs. — 3. dbugs rtségs-pa (dbugs rtsegs pa), gyén-du dbugs(-kyis) rtség-pa (gyen du dbugs (-kyis) rtseg pa) Med., short-breathed, asthmatic, panting, gasping, from fright etc., or as a sign of approaching death. — dkon(-mčóg) brtségs(-pa) (dkon (-mchog) brtsegs (-pa)); रत्नकूट title of a book.
pf. brtseṅs (brtsengs), fut. brtseṅ (brtseng), imp. (b)rtsoṅ(s) ((b)rtsong (s)) to tuck up, truss up.
I. also rtséṅ-pa (rtseng pa), = rtsé-ba (rtse ba) to play; rtsed rtsé-ba (rtsed rtse ba) id.; rtséd-mo (rtsed mo)
